recommendations: Adopt a modern `pyproject.toml` build configuration (PEP 517/621) while keeping `setup.py` compatibility during transition
Add a minimal smoke-test CI matrix for supported Python versions and key optional dependency sets (e.g.
bare
scientific
full) since test status is currently empty
Reduce MCP plugin surface area by exposing only curated high-value/stable endpoints instead of thousands of auto-exported symbols
Add endpoint namespacing and deduplication in MCP generation (many collisions like `demo`
`update`
class/function name overlaps)
Introduce API stability tiers (public/experimental/internal) and generate MCP tools only from public tier
Add contract tests for MCP endpoints (signature validation
importability
deterministic output
error schema)
Implement timeout/guardrails for heavy NLP operations in MCP service to prevent long-running calls from blocking workers
Add structured logging and tracing in `mcp_service.py` with per-endpoint latency/error metrics
Add type hints and run `mypy` (or pyright) incrementally on core modules plus MCP adapter layer
Strengthen security posture for exposed file/network-sensitive utilities (path allowlists
SSRF-safe URL handling
subprocess restrictions)
Create a “recommended install profiles” doc (`minimal`
`ml`
`viz`
`twitter`) aligned to optional deps
Add repository-level architectural docs mapping major NLTK subsystems to maintainability owners
Prioritize refactors for very large legacy modules (e.g.
downloader/data/grammar/featstruct) by extracting cohesive helper submodules
Enforce consistent lint/format rules in CI (ruff/black/isort) and fail fast on style regressions
Add performance benchmarks for tokenizer/tagger/downloader hot paths and track regressions in CI
Improve test data management by marking large/slow/integration tests explicitly and splitting quick unit tests from resource-dependent tests
Add CLI integration tests for `python -m nltk` and `python -m nltk.downloader` with mocked network/data dirs
Publish a machine-readable deprecation registry and validate it with `tools/find_deprecated.py` in CI
Add release automation checks for changelog completeness
API diff summary
and wheel/sdist verification
Improve `README` with clearer quickstart for modern usage plus explicit corpus download/security notes
Add failure-tolerant DeepWiki/analysis fallback reporting so meta-analysis failures are visible but non-blocking with actionable diagnostics

success_factors: Workflow reached terminal success state across all nodes (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
Import-based adapter mode had high feasibility (0.9) for NLTK’s Python package structure.
Low intrusiveness risk and medium complexity profile supported automated generation without blocking errors.
MCP plugin runtime validation passed with healthy service status over stdio transport.
failure_reasons: DeepWiki analysis failed (non-blocking) and reduced external contextual enrichment.
Original project test result metadata is inconsistent/empty despite large test surface, limiting confidence in compatibility validation.
Generated artifact metrics (LOC/size/tool_endpoints reported as 0) conflict with actual large endpoint list, indicating observability/reporting defects.

node_performance.download_time: Completed successfully via zip fallback; likely fast-to-moderate. Exact node timing not provided.
node_performance.analysis_time: Most expensive phase likely due to large AST scan (~399 files) and endpoint discovery; exact timing unavailable.
node_performance.generation_time: Completed successfully and produced required MCP scaffold files; timing not isolated.
node_performance.test_time: MCP service health check passed quickly; original-project test execution effectively not performed/recorded.

resource_usage.memory_efficiency: Unknown from telemetry (reported 0 MB), but likely acceptable for static analysis + codegen workload.
resource_usage.cpu_efficiency: Unknown from telemetry (reported 0%); analysis stage likely CPU-dominant due to large symbol extraction.
resource_usage.disk_usage: Low-to-moderate additional footprint (generated MCP files only), though reported size=0 indicates metrics collection issue.
